This ensures that the document has not been tampered with since it was signed. Any modification to the content will invalidate the signature. Digital signatures use cryptographic hash functions to create a unique fingerprint of the document. Any changes to the document will alter the hash value, making the signature invalid

This verifies the identity of the signer, ensuring the document originated from a trusted source Digital signatures rely on public key cryptography. The signer uses their private key to create the signature, and the recipient uses the corresponding public key to verify it. This prevents the signer from denying that they signed the document. Once a valid signature is created, it's cryptographically linked to the signer.
